;; Brute-force SAT solver for the given 3-CNF formula
;; Variables: x1 x2 x3 x4 x5
;; Clauses:
;;  1. (¬x4 ∨  x1 ∨ ¬x2)
;;  2. ( x5 ∨  x2 ∨  x1)
;;  3. ( x4 ∨ ¬x3 ∨  x5)
;;  4. (¬x2 ∨ ¬x5 ∨ ¬x1)
;;  5. (¬x1 ∨  x3 ∨  x5)
;;  6. (¬x3 ∨  x4 ∨  x1)
;;  7. ( x2 ∨  x1 ∨ ¬x5)
;;  8. (¬x2 ∨  x5 ∨  x4)
;;  9. (¬x4 ∨ ¬x3 ∨  x5)
;; 10. (¬x5 ∨ ¬x3 ∨ ¬x4)

(define (check x1 x2 x3 x4 x5)
  (and
    (or (not x4) x1 (not x2))        ; clause 1
    (or x5 x2 x1)                    ; clause 2
    (or x4 (not x3) x5)              ; clause 3
    (or (not x2) (not x5) (not x1))  ; clause 4
    (or (not x1) x3 x5)              ; clause 5
    (or (not x3) x4 x1)              ; clause 6
    (or x2 x1 (not x5))              ; clause 7
    (or (not x2) x5 x4)              ; clause 8
    (or (not x4) (not x3) x5)        ; clause 9
    (or (not x5) (not x3) (not x4))  ; clause 10
  ))

(define result
  (let loop ((i 0))
    (if (= i 32)
        'UNSAT
        (let* ((x1 (= (bitwise-and i 16) 16))
               (x2 (= (bitwise-and i  8)  8))
               (x3 (= (bitwise-and i  4)  4))
               (x4 (= (bitwise-and i  2)  2))
               (x5 (= (bitwise-and i  1)  1)))
          (if (check x1 x2 x3 x4 x5)
              (list x1 x2 x3 x4 x5)
              (loop (+ i 1)))))))

result

(import (scheme base))
(define (bit-set? n bit) (not (= 0 (modulo (floor-quotient n (expt 2 bit)) 2))))

(define (check x1 x2 x3 x4 x5)
  (and
    (or (not x4) x1 (not x2))
    (or x5 x2 x1)
    (or x4 (not x3) x5)
    (or (not x2) (not x5) (not x1))
    (or (not x1) x3 x5)
    (or (not x3) x4 x1)
    (or x2 x1 (not x5))
    (or (not x2) x5 x4)
    (or (not x4) (not x3) x5)
    (or (not x5) (not x3) (not x4))))

(let loop ((i 0))
  (if (= i 32)
      (display "UNSAT")
      (let* ((x1 (bit-set? i 4))
             (x2 (bit-set? i 3))
             (x3 (bit-set? i 2))
             (x4 (bit-set? i 1))
             (x5 (bit-set? i 0)))
        (if (check x1 x2 x3 x4 x5)
            (begin
              (display "SAT: ")
              (display (list 'x1= x1 'x2= x2 'x3= x3 'x4= x4 'x5= x5)))
            (loop (+ i 1))))))

; Encode the formula as a list of clauses
; Each clause is a list of literals: positive integer = variable true, negative = variable false
; Variables: x1=1, x2=2, x3=3, x4=4, x5=5

; Clauses:
; (¬x4 ∨ x3 ∨ x5)
; (x3 ∨ ¬x4 ∨ x2)
; (¬x2 ∨ x1 ∨ x4)
; (x5 ∨ ¬x2 ∨ ¬x1)
; (¬x4 ∨ ¬x3 ∨ ¬x5)
; (¬x1 ∨ x4 ∨ ¬x5)
; (x4 ∨ ¬x2 ∨ x5)
; (x4 ∨ x3 ∨ ¬x1)
; (¬x1 ∨ x2 ∨ ¬x5)
; (¬x3 ∨ x4 ∨ x1)

(define clauses
  '((-4 3 5)
    (3 -4 2)
    (-2 1 4)
    (5 -2 -1)
    (-4 -3 -5)
    (-1 4 -5)
    (4 -2 5)
    (4 3 -1)
    (-1 2 -5)
    (-3 4 1)))

; Evaluate a literal given an assignment vector (1-indexed)
(define (lit-val lit assignment)
  (if (> lit 0)
      (vector-ref assignment lit)
      (not (vector-ref assignment (- lit)))))

; Evaluate a clause (OR of literals)
(define (clause-sat? clause assignment)
  (let loop ((lits clause))
    (cond
      ((null? lits) #f)
      ((lit-val (car lits) assignment) #t)
      (else (loop (cdr lits))))))

; Evaluate the formula (AND of clauses)
(define (formula-sat? assignment)
  (let loop ((cls clauses))
    (cond
      ((null? cls) #t)
      ((not (clause-sat? (car cls) assignment)) #f)
      (else (loop (cdr cls))))))

; Try all 2^5 = 32 assignments
(define (solve)
  (let loop ((i 0))
    (if (= i 32)
        'UNSAT
        (let* ((x1 (= (bitwise-and i 1) 1))
               (x2 (= (bitwise-and i 2) 2))
               (x3 (= (bitwise-and i 4) 4))
               (x4 (= (bitwise-and i 8) 8))
               (x5 (= (bitwise-and i 16) 16))
               (assignment (vector #f x1 x2 x3 x4 x5)))
          (if (formula-sat? assignment)
              (list 'x1= x1 'x2= x2 'x3= x3 'x4= x4 'x5= x5)
              (loop (+ i 1)))))))

;; Represent each clause as a list of literals
;; Positive literal: (var . #t), Negative literal: (var . #f)
;; Variables x1..x8 indexed 1..8

(define clauses
  '(
    ((1 . #t)  (8 . #t)  (2 . #f))   ; x1 ∨ x8 ∨ ¬x2
    ((3 . #f)  (4 . #f)  (2 . #f))   ; ¬x3 ∨ ¬x4 ∨ ¬x2
    ((6 . #f)  (7 . #f)  (8 . #f))   ; ¬x6 ∨ ¬x7 ∨ ¬x8
    ((1 . #t)  (4 . #f)  (8 . #f))   ; x1 ∨ ¬x4 ∨ ¬x8
    ((7 . #f)  (4 . #t)  (2 . #f))   ; ¬x7 ∨ x4 ∨ ¬x2
    ((2 . #f)  (4 . #t)  (6 . #t))   ; ¬x2 ∨ x4 ∨ x6
    ((6 . #t)  (8 . #t)  (4 . #t))   ; x6 ∨ x8 ∨ x4
    ((1 . #f)  (4 . #f)  (5 . #f))   ; ¬x1 ∨ ¬x4 ∨ ¬x5
    ((5 . #t)  (7 . #t)  (1 . #f))   ; x5 ∨ x7 ∨ ¬x1
    ((7 . #f)  (4 . #f)  (1 . #f))   ; ¬x7 ∨ ¬x4 ∨ ¬x1
    ((1 . #f)  (3 . #t)  (8 . #f))   ; ¬x1 ∨ x3 ∨ ¬x8
    ((3 . #f)  (1 . #t)  (7 . #t))   ; ¬x3 ∨ x1 ∨ x7
    ((7 . #f)  (5 . #t)  (6 . #t))   ; ¬x7 ∨ x5 ∨ x6
    ((1 . #t)  (5 . #f)  (2 . #t))   ; x1 ∨ ¬x5 ∨ x2
    ((2 . #f)  (5 . #t)  (8 . #f))   ; ¬x2 ∨ x5 ∨ ¬x8
    ((2 . #t)  (8 . #t)  (5 . #f))   ; x2 ∨ x8 ∨ ¬x5
    ((7 . #f)  (3 . #f)  (2 . #t))   ; ¬x7 ∨ ¬x3 ∨ x2
    ((2 . #t)  (4 . #f)  (6 . #t))   ; x2 ∨ ¬x4 ∨ x6
  ))

;; Evaluate a clause under assignment (vector of booleans, 1-indexed)
(define (eval-clause clause assignment)
  (let loop ((lits clause))
    (if (null? lits)
        #f
        (let* ((lit (car lits))
               (var (car lit))
               (pol (cdr lit))
               (val (vector-ref assignment var)))
          (if (eq? val pol)
              #t
              (loop (cdr lits)))))))

;; Evaluate all clauses
(define (eval-formula assignment)
  (let loop ((cls clauses))
    (if (null? cls)
        #t
        (if (eval-clause (car cls) assignment)
            (loop (cdr cls))
            #f))))

;; Try all 2^8 = 256 assignments
(define (solve)
  (let loop ((i 0))
    (if (= i 256)
        'UNSAT
        (let ((assignment (make-vector 9 #f)))
          ;; Unpack bits into x1..x8
          (do ((bit 0 (+ bit 1)))
              ((= bit 8))
            (vector-set! assignment (+ bit 1)
                         (not (= 0 (bitwise-and i (arithmetic-shift 1 bit))))))
          (if (eval-formula assignment)
              assignment
              (loop (+ i 1)))))))

(define result (solve))

(if (eq? result 'UNSAT)
    (display "UNSAT")
    (begin
      (display "SAT: ")
      (do ((v 1 (+ v 1)))
          ((> v 8))
        (display (string-append "x" (number->string v) "="))
        (display (if (vector-ref result v) "T" "F"))
        (display " "))))
(newline)

;; Use modulo and quotient instead of bitwise ops
(define clauses
  '(
    ((1 . #t)  (8 . #t)  (2 . #f))
    ((3 . #f)  (4 . #f)  (2 . #f))
    ((6 . #f)  (7 . #f)  (8 . #f))
    ((1 . #t)  (4 . #f)  (8 . #f))
    ((7 . #f)  (4 . #t)  (2 . #f))
    ((2 . #f)  (4 . #t)  (6 . #t))
    ((6 . #t)  (8 . #t)  (4 . #t))
    ((1 . #f)  (4 . #f)  (5 . #f))
    ((5 . #t)  (7 . #t)  (1 . #f))
    ((7 . #f)  (4 . #f)  (1 . #f))
    ((1 . #f)  (3 . #t)  (8 . #f))
    ((3 . #f)  (1 . #t)  (7 . #t))
    ((7 . #f)  (5 . #t)  (6 . #t))
    ((1 . #t)  (5 . #f)  (2 . #t))
    ((2 . #f)  (5 . #t)  (8 . #f))
    ((2 . #t)  (8 . #t)  (5 . #f))
    ((7 . #f)  (3 . #f)  (2 . #t))
    ((2 . #t)  (4 . #f)  (6 . #t))
  ))

(define (int->bits n)
  ;; returns vector of 9 elements (1-indexed), bits for x1..x8
  (let ((v (make-vector 9 #f)))
    (let loop ((k 0) (m n))
      (if (= k 8)
          v
          (begin
            (vector-set! v (+ k 1) (= 1 (modulo m 2)))
            (loop (+ k 1) (quotient m 2)))))))

(define (eval-clause clause assignment)
  (let loop ((lits clause))
    (if (null? lits)
        #f
        (let* ((lit (car lits))
               (var (car lit))
               (pol (cdr lit))
               (val (vector-ref assignment var)))
          (if (eq? val pol) #t (loop (cdr lits)))))))

(define (eval-formula assignment)
  (let loop ((cls clauses))
    (if (null? cls) #t
        (if (eval-clause (car cls) assignment)
            (loop (cdr cls))
            #f))))

(define (solve)
  (let loop ((i 0))
    (if (= i 256)
        'UNSAT
        (let ((assignment (int->bits i)))
          (if (eval-formula assignment)
              (cons i assignment)
              (loop (+ i 1)))))))

(define result (solve))

(if (eq? result 'UNSAT)
    (display "UNSAT")
    (let ((assignment (cdr result)))
      (display "SAT: ")
      (do ((v 1 (+ v 1)))
          ((> v 8))
        (display (string-append "x" (number->string v) "="))
        (display (if (vector-ref assignment v) "T" "F"))
        (display " "))))
(newline)

;; Encode the 3-SAT problem and try all 2^8 = 256 assignments
;; Variables: x1..x8, represented as a vector of booleans
;; Positive literal k means x_k is true, negative means x_k is false

(define (literal-val vars lit)
  ;; lit > 0: variable index (1-based), lit < 0: negated variable
  (if (> lit 0)
      (vector-ref vars (- lit 1))
      (not (vector-ref vars (- (- lit) 1)))))

(define (clause-sat? vars clause)
  (or (literal-val vars (car clause))
      (literal-val vars (cadr clause))
      (literal-val vars (caddr clause))))

(define clauses
  '((−3  5  8)   ; (¬x3 ∨ x5 ∨ x8)
    ( 8 −6 −3)   ; (x8 ∨ ¬x6 ∨ ¬x3)
    ( 8 −3 −1)   ; (x8 ∨ ¬x3 ∨ ¬x1)
    (−1  3 −4)   ; (¬x1 ∨ x3 ∨ ¬x4)
    ( 6  5  7)   ; (x6 ∨ x5 ∨ x7)
    (−5 −4  6)   ; (¬x5 ∨ ¬x4 ∨ x6)
    ( 7  3  1)   ; (x7 ∨ x3 ∨ x1)
    (−7  5 −2)   ; (¬x7 ∨ x5 ∨ ¬x2)
    ( 5  7  1)   ; (x5 ∨ x7 ∨ x1)
    (−2  5  8)   ; (¬x2 ∨ x5 ∨ x8)
    (−7  8  2)   ; (¬x7 ∨ x8 ∨ x2)
    (−5 −7  2)   ; (¬x5 ∨ ¬x7 ∨ x2)
    (−3 −6  4)   ; (¬x3 ∨ ¬x6 ∨ x4)
    ( 1  3 −5)   ; (x1 ∨ x3 ∨ ¬x5)
    (−3  5  8)   ; (¬x3 ∨ x5 ∨ x8)
    (−1 −8 −6)   ; (¬x1 ∨ ¬x8 ∨ ¬x6)
    (−2  6 −1)   ; (¬x2 ∨ x6 ∨ ¬x1)
    (−8  5  6))) ; (¬x8 ∨ x5 ∨ x6)

(define (formula-sat? vars)
  (let loop ((cs clauses))
    (if (null? cs)
        #t
        (and (clause-sat? vars (car cs))
             (loop (cdr cs))))))

;; Try all 256 assignments
(let search ((i 0))
  (if (= i 256)
      (display "UNSAT")
      (let* ((vars (vector
                     (odd? (arithmetic-shift i -7))
                     (odd? (arithmetic-shift i -6))

                     (odd? (arithmetic-shift i -3))

                  (display " ")
                  (pr (+ k 1)))))
            (search (+ i 1))))))

  ;; check if bit k (0-based) of n is set
  (odd? (quotient n (expt 2 k))))

(define (literal-val vars lit)
  (if (> lit 0)
      (vector-ref vars (- lit 1))
      (not (vector-ref vars (- (- lit) 1)))))

(define (clause-sat? vars clause)
  (or (literal-val vars (car clause))
      (literal-val vars (cadr clause))
      (literal-val vars (caddr clause))))

(define clauses
  (list
    (list -3  5  8)
    (list  8 -6 -3)
    (list  8 -3 -1)

    (list  1  3 -5)
    (list -3  5  8)
    (list -1 -8 -6)
    (list -2  6 -1)
    (list -8  5  6)))

(define (formula-sat? vars)
  (let loop ((cs clauses))
    (if (null? cs)
        #t
        (and (clause-sat? vars (car cs))
             (loop (cdr cs))))))

;; Try all 256 assignments

(let search ((i 0))

;; Represent each clause as a list of (var-index . polarity) pairs
;; polarity #t = positive, #f = negative
;; Variables x_1..x_8 indexed 0..7

(define (make-assignment bits)
  ;; bits is an integer 0..255, bit i = value of x_{i+1}
  (lambda (i) (not (= 0 (bitwise-and bits (arithmetic-shift 1 i))))))

(define clauses
  ;; (x_7 ∨ ¬x_5 ∨ ¬x_1)
  '(((6 . #t) (4 . #f) (0 . #f))
    ;; (¬x_4 ∨ x_2 ∨ x_3)
    ((3 . #f) (1 . #t) (2 . #t))
    ;; (¬x_3 ∨ ¬x_8 ∨ ¬x_4)
    ((2 . #f) (7 . #f) (3 . #f))
    ;; (x_2 ∨ x_8 ∨ x_4)
    ((1 . #t) (7 . #t) (3 . #t))
    ;; (¬x_2 ∨ x_3 ∨ ¬x_6)
    ((1 . #f) (2 . #t) (5 . #f))
    ;; (¬x_1 ∨ ¬x_3 ∨ x_5)
    ((0 . #f) (2 . #f) (4 . #t))
    ;; (x_5 ∨ ¬x_4 ∨ ¬x_2)
    ((4 . #t) (3 . #f) (1 . #f))
    ;; (x_2 ∨ ¬x_3 ∨ ¬x_4)
    ((1 . #t) (2 . #f) (3 . #f))
    ;; (x_5 ∨ ¬x_3 ∨ x_4)
    ((4 . #t) (2 . #f) (3 . #t))
    ;; (¬x_2 ∨ x_8 ∨ ¬x_1)
    ((1 . #f) (7 . #t) (0 . #f))
    ;; (¬x_3 ∨ x_7 ∨ ¬x_4)
    ((2 . #f) (6 . #t) (3 . #f))
    ;; (¬x_4 ∨ ¬x_8 ∨ ¬x_7)
    ((3 . #f) (7 . #f) (6 . #f))
    ;; (x_5 ∨ ¬x_3 ∨ ¬x_1)
    ((4 . #t) (2 . #f) (0 . #f))
    ;; (¬x_6 ∨ x_3 ∨ ¬x_7)
    ((5 . #f) (2 . #t) (6 . #f))
    ;; (¬x_2 ∨ x_1 ∨ x_7)
    ((1 . #f) (0 . #t) (6 . #t))
    ;; (x_3 ∨ x_7 ∨ x_5)
    ((2 . #t) (6 . #t) (4 . #t))
    ;; (x_1 ∨ x_4 ∨ x_6)
    ((0 . #t) (3 . #t) (5 . #t))
    ;; (x_2 ∨ x_1 ∨ x_8)
    ((1 . #t) (0 . #t) (7 . #t))))

(define (eval-clause assign clause)
  (let loop ((lits clause))
    (if (null? lits)
        #f
        (let* ((lit (car lits))
               (idx (car lit))
               (pol (cdr lit))
               (val (assign idx)))
          (if (eq? val pol)
              #t
              (loop (cdr lits)))))))

(define (eval-formula assign)
  (let loop ((cs clauses))
    (if (null? cs)
        #t
        (if (eval-clause assign (car cs))
            (loop (cdr cs))
            #f))))

;; Brute force search
(let loop ((bits 0))
  (if (>= bits 256)
      (display "UNSAT")
      (let ((assign (make-assignment bits)))
        (if (eval-formula assign)
            (begin
              (display "SAT: ")
              (do ((i 0 (+ i 1)))
                  ((= i 8))
                (display (string-append "x" (number->string (+ i 1)) "="))
                (display (if (assign i) "T" "F"))
                (display " ")))
            (loop (+ bits 1))))))

;; Encode the 3-SAT formula and check satisfiability by brute force
;; Variables: x1..x8, represented as a list of booleans (index 0 = x1, etc.)

(define (check-formula vars)
  ;; vars is a vector of 8 booleans, index 0=x1 ... 7=x8
  (let ((x1 (vector-ref vars 0))
        (x2 (vector-ref vars 1))
        (x3 (vector-ref vars 2))
        (x4 (vector-ref vars 3))
        (x5 (vector-ref vars 4))
        (x6 (vector-ref vars 5))
        (x7 (vector-ref vars 6))
        (x8 (vector-ref vars 7)))
    (and
      ;; (¬x5 ∨ ¬x7 ∨ x6)
      (or (not x5) (not x7) x6)
      ;; (¬x5 ∨ x1 ∨ ¬x6)
      (or (not x5) x1 (not x6))
      ;; (¬x4 ∨ x5 ∨ x7)
      (or (not x4) x5 x7)
      ;; (x3 ∨ x7 ∨ ¬x8)
      (or x3 x7 (not x8))
      ;; (¬x5 ∨ ¬x8 ∨ x7)
      (or (not x5) (not x8) x7)
      ;; (x4 ∨ x6 ∨ ¬x5)
      (or x4 x6 (not x5))
      ;; (¬x2 ∨ ¬x5 ∨ ¬x6)
      (or (not x2) (not x5) (not x6))
      ;; (¬x7 ∨ ¬x5 ∨ ¬x6)
      (or (not x7) (not x5) (not x6))
      ;; (x5 ∨ x3 ∨ ¬x2)
      (or x5 x3 (not x2))
      ;; (¬x7 ∨ ¬x8 ∨ x4)
      (or (not x7) (not x8) x4)
      ;; (x2 ∨ ¬x4 ∨ ¬x7)
      (or x2 (not x4) (not x7))
      ;; (¬x8 ∨ x5 ∨ x4)
      (or (not x8) x5 x4)
      ;; (¬x4 ∨ ¬x5 ∨ x2)
      (or (not x4) (not x5) x2)
      ;; (¬x5 ∨ ¬x4 ∨ x2)
      (or (not x5) (not x4) x2)
      ;; (¬x6 ∨ ¬x7 ∨ x2)
      (or (not x6) (not x7) x2)
      ;; (x3 ∨ ¬x8 ∨ ¬x6)
      (or x3 (not x8) (not x6))
      ;; (x1 ∨ x7 ∨ x6)
      (or x1 x7 x6)
      ;; (¬x6 ∨ ¬x5 ∨ ¬x8)
      (or (not x6) (not x5) (not x8)))))

;; Enumerate all 2^8 = 256 assignments
(define (solve)
  (let loop ((i 0))
    (if (= i 256)
        (display "UNSAT\n")
        (let* ((bits (list (not (= 0 (bitwise-and i 1)))
                           (not (= 0 (bitwise-and i 2)))
                           (not (= 0 (bitwise-and i 4)))
                           (not (= 0 (bitwise-and i 8)))
                           (not (= 0 (bitwise-and i 16)))
                           (not (= 0 (bitwise-and i 32)))
                           (not (= 0 (bitwise-and i 64)))
                           (not (= 0 (bitwise-and i 128)))))
               (vars (list->vector bits)))
          (if (check-formula vars)
              (begin
                (display "SAT: ")
                (let show ((k 1) (b bits))
                  (when (not (null? b))
                    (display (string-append "x" (number->string k) "="))
                    (display (if (car b) "T" "F"))
                    (display " ")
                    (show (+ k 1) (cdr b))))
                (newline))
              (loop (+ i 1)))))))

(solve)

;; Use modulo/quotient instead of bitwise operations
(define (bit-set? n k)
  ;; Is bit k set in n?
  (odd? (quotient n (expt 2 k))))

(define (check-formula vars)
  (let ((x1 (vector-ref vars 0))
        (x2 (vector-ref vars 1))
        (x3 (vector-ref vars 2))
        (x4 (vector-ref vars 3))
        (x5 (vector-ref vars 4))
        (x6 (vector-ref vars 5))
        (x7 (vector-ref vars 6))
        (x8 (vector-ref vars 7)))
    (and
      (or (not x5) (not x7) x6)
      (or (not x5) x1 (not x6))
      (or (not x4) x5 x7)
      (or x3 x7 (not x8))
      (or (not x5) (not x8) x7)
      (or x4 x6 (not x5))
      (or (not x2) (not x5) (not x6))
      (or (not x7) (not x5) (not x6))
      (or x5 x3 (not x2))
      (or (not x7) (not x8) x4)
      (or x2 (not x4) (not x7))
      (or (not x8) x5 x4)
      (or (not x4) (not x5) x2)
      (or (not x5) (not x4) x2)
      (or (not x6) (not x7) x2)
      (or x3 (not x8) (not x6))
      (or x1 x7 x6)
      (or (not x6) (not x5) (not x8)))))

(define (solve)
  (let loop ((i 0))
    (if (= i 256)
        (display "UNSAT\n")
        (let* ((bits (map (lambda (k) (bit-set? i k)) '(0 1 2 3 4 5 6 7)))
               (vars (list->vector bits)))
          (if (check-formula vars)
              (begin
                (display "SAT: ")
                (let show ((k 1) (b bits))
                  (when (not (null? b))
                    (display (string-append "x" (number->string k) "="))
                    (display (if (car b) "T" "F"))
                    (display " ")
                    (show (+ k 1) (cdr b))))
                (newline))
              (loop (+ i 1)))))))

(solve)

;; Encode the 3-SAT formula
;; Variables: x1..x8, represented as indices 1..8
;; Each clause is a list of literals: positive = var index, negative = negated

;; Clauses from the formula:
(define clauses
  '((7 1 5)        ; (x7 ∨ x1 ∨ x5)
    (6 8 5)        ; (x6 ∨ x8 ∨ x5)
    (7 2 -1)       ; (x7 ∨ x2 ∨ ¬x1)
    (1 8 4)        ; (x1 ∨ x8 ∨ x4)
    (2 6 5)        ; (x2 ∨ x6 ∨ x5)
    (5 4 -7)       ; (x5 ∨ x4 ∨ ¬x7)
    (-8 -4 -7)     ; (¬x8 ∨ ¬x4 ∨ ¬x7)
    (-7 -1 -3)     ; (¬x7 ∨ ¬x1 ∨ ¬x3)
    (8 -2 4)       ; (x8 ∨ ¬x2 ∨ x4)
    (-5 3 -6)      ; (¬x5 ∨ x3 ∨ ¬x6)
    (-6 7 -8)      ; (¬x6 ∨ x7 ∨ ¬x8)
    (3 6 -5)       ; (x3 ∨ x6 ∨ ¬x5)
    (-4 -6 -7)     ; (¬x4 ∨ ¬x6 ∨ ¬x7)
    (-4 1 5)       ; (¬x4 ∨ x1 ∨ x5)
    (-4 -7 5)      ; (¬x4 ∨ ¬x7 ∨ x5)
    (-1 2 -3)      ; (¬x1 ∨ x2 ∨ ¬x3)
    (-4 8 -7)      ; (¬x4 ∨ x8 ∨ ¬x7)
    (-7 -4 -2)))   ; (¬x7 ∨ ¬x4 ∨ ¬x2)

;; Evaluate a literal given an assignment (vector of 9 booleans, index 1..8)
(define (lit-val lit assignment)
  (if (> lit 0)
      (vector-ref assignment lit)
      (not (vector-ref assignment (- lit)))))

;; Evaluate a clause
(define (clause-sat? clause assignment)
  (any (lambda (lit) (lit-val lit assignment)) clause))

;; Evaluate the whole formula
(define (formula-sat? assignment)
  (every (lambda (clause) (clause-sat? clause assignment)) clauses))

;; Brute-force search over all 2^8 = 256 assignments
(define (search)
  (let loop ((i 0))
    (if (> i 255)
        #f
        (let ((assignment (make-vector 9 #f)))
          ;; Set bits
          (do ((v 1) (bit 0 (+ bit 1)))
              ((> v 8))
            (vector-set! assignment v (= 1 (bitwise-and 1 (arithmetic-shift i (- bit)))))
            (set! v (+ v 1)))
          ;; Actually let me redo this more carefully
          (let ((a (make-vector 9 #f)))
            (vector-set! a 1 (= 1 (bitwise-and (arithmetic-shift i -0) 1)))
            (vector-set! a 2 (= 1 (bitwise-and (arithmetic-shift i -1) 1)))
            (vector-set! a 3 (= 1 (bitwise-and (arithmetic-shift i -2) 1)))
            (vector-set! a 4 (= 1 (bitwise-and (arithmetic-shift i -3) 1)))
            (vector-set! a 5 (= 1 (bitwise-and (arithmetic-shift i -4) 1)))
            (vector-set! a 6 (= 1 (bitwise-and (arithmetic-shift i -5) 1)))
            (vector-set! a 7 (= 1 (bitwise-and (arithmetic-shift i -6) 1)))
            (vector-set! a 8 (= 1 (bitwise-and (arithmetic-shift i -7) 1)))
            (if (formula-sat? a)
                a
                (loop (+ i 1))))))))

(define result (search))
(if result
    (begin
      (display "SAT: ")
      (do ((v 1 (+ v 1)))
          ((> v 8))
        (display (string-append "x" (number->string v) "="))
        (display (if (vector-ref result v) "T" "F"))
        (display " "))
      (newline))
    (display "UNSAT"))

;; Redefine using explicit implementations
(define clauses
  '((7 1 5)
    (6 8 5)
    (7 2 -1)
    (1 8 4)
    (2 6 5)
    (5 4 -7)
    (-8 -4 -7)
    (-7 -1 -3)
    (8 -2 4)
    (-5 3 -6)
    (-6 7 -8)
    (3 6 -5)
    (-4 -6 -7)
    (-4 1 5)
    (-4 -7 5)
    (-1 2 -3)
    (-4 8 -7)
    (-7 -4 -2)))

(define (lit-val lit a)
  (if (> lit 0)
      (vector-ref a lit)
      (not (vector-ref a (- lit)))))

(define (clause-sat? clause a)
  (let loop ((lits clause))
    (cond ((null? lits) #f)
          ((lit-val (car lits) a) #t)
          (else (loop (cdr lits))))))

(define (formula-sat? a)
  (let loop ((cs clauses))
    (cond ((null? cs) #t)
          ((not (clause-sat? (car cs) a)) #f)
          (else (loop (cdr cs))))))

(define (search)
  (let loop ((i 0))
    (if (> i 255)
        #f
        (let ((a (make-vector 9 #f)))
          (vector-set! a 1 (= 1 (bitwise-and (arithmetic-shift i  0) 1)))
          (vector-set! a 2 (= 1 (bitwise-and (arithmetic-shift i -1) 1)))
          (vector-set! a 3 (= 1 (bitwise-and (arithmetic-shift i -2) 1)))
          (vector-set! a 4 (= 1 (bitwise-and (arithmetic-shift i -3) 1)))
          (vector-set! a 5 (= 1 (bitwise-and (arithmetic-shift i -4) 1)))
          (vector-set! a 6 (= 1 (bitwise-and (arithmetic-shift i -5) 1)))
          (vector-set! a 7 (= 1 (bitwise-and (arithmetic-shift i -6) 1)))
          (vector-set! a 8 (= 1 (bitwise-and (arithmetic-shift i -7) 1)))
          (if (formula-sat? a)
              a
              (loop (+ i 1)))))))

(define result (search))
(if result
    (begin
      (display "SAT: ")
      (let loop ((v 1))
        (when (<= v 8)
          (display (string-append "x" (number->string v) "="))
          (display (if (vector-ref result v) "T" "F"))
          (display " ")
          (loop (+ v 1))))
      (newline)
      ;; Verify
      (display "Verification: ")
      (display (formula-sat? result)))
    (display "UNSAT"))

;; Let's check what's available for integer operations
(import (scheme base))
(display (quotient 7 2))
(newline)
(display (remainder 7 2))

;; Use quotient/remainder instead of bitwise ops
(define clauses
  '((7 1 5)
    (6 8 5)
    (7 2 -1)
    (1 8 4)
    (2 6 5)
    (5 4 -7)
    (-8 -4 -7)
    (-7 -1 -3)
    (8 -2 4)
    (-5 3 -6)
    (-6 7 -8)
    (3 6 -5)
    (-4 -6 -7)
    (-4 1 5)
    (-4 -7 5)
    (-1 2 -3)
    (-4 8 -7)
    (-7 -4 -2)))

(define (bit i k)
  ;; return the k-th bit (0-indexed) of integer i
  (remainder (quotient i (expt 2 k)) 2))

(define (lit-val lit a)
  (if (> lit 0)
      (vector-ref a lit)
      (not (vector-ref a (- lit)))))

(define (clause-sat? clause a)
  (let loop ((lits clause))
    (cond ((null? lits) #f)
          ((lit-val (car lits) a) #t)
          (else (loop (cdr lits))))))

(define (formula-sat? a)
  (let loop ((cs clauses))
    (cond ((null? cs) #t)
          ((not (clause-sat? (car cs) a)) #f)
          (else (loop (cdr cs))))))

(define (make-assignment i)
  (let ((a (make-vector 9 #f)))
    (vector-set! a 1 (= 1 (bit i 0)))
    (vector-set! a 2 (= 1 (bit i 1)))
    (vector-set! a 3 (= 1 (bit i 2)))
    (vector-set! a 4 (= 1 (bit i 3)))
    (vector-set! a 5 (= 1 (bit i 4)))
    (vector-set! a 6 (= 1 (bit i 5)))
    (vector-set! a 7 (= 1 (bit i 6)))
    (vector-set! a 8 (= 1 (bit i 7)))
    a))

(define (search)
  (let loop ((i 0))
    (if (> i 255)
        #f
        (let ((a (make-assignment i)))
          (if (formula-sat? a)
              a
              (loop (+ i 1)))))))

(define result (search))
(if result
    (begin
      (display "SAT: ")
      (let loop ((v 1))
        (when (<= v 8)
          (display (string-append "x" (number->string v) "="))
          (display (if (vector-ref result v) "T" "F"))
          (display " ")
          (loop (+ v 1))))
      (newline))
    (display "UNSAT"))

arguments.code:
;; Represent a permutation as a vector: index i maps to sigma[i]
;; σ = (1 4 3 2) means 1->4, 4->3, 3->2, 2->1 (cycle notation)
;; Using 1-based indexing stored at positions 1..4

(define (make-perm . mapping)
  ;; mapping is a list of pairs (i . j) meaning i -> j
  (let ((v (vector 0 1 2 3 4))) ;; identity by default
    (for-each (lambda (pair) (vector-set! v (car pair) (cdr pair))) mapping)
    v))

;; Compose permutations: (compose p q) means first apply q then p (or p∘q)
;; Actually let's define apply-perm: apply p to element x
(define (apply-perm p x) (vector-ref p x))

;; Compose: (p ∘ q)(x) = p(q(x))
(define (compose-perm p q)
  (let ((v (make-vector 5 0)))
    (do ((i 1 (+ i 1)))
        ((> i 4) v)
      (vector-set! v i (apply-perm p (apply-perm q i))))))

;; Identity permutation
(define identity-perm (vector 0 1 2 3 4))

(define (perm=? p q)
  (let loop ((i 1))
    (if (> i 4) #t
        (if (= (vector-ref p i) (vector-ref q i))
            (loop (+ i 1))
            #f))))

;; σ = (1 4 3 2): cycle 1->4->3->2->1
(define sigma (make-perm '(1 . 4) '(4 . 3) '(3 . 2) '(2 . 1)))

;; Display sigma
(display "sigma: ") (display sigma) (newline)

;; Compute powers until we get identity
(let loop ((k 1) (current sigma))
  (display "sigma^") (display k) (display " = ") (display current) (newline)
  (if (perm=? current identity-perm)
      (begin (display "Order of sigma = ") (display k) (newline))
      (if (> k 10)
          (display "Not found within 10 iterations")
          (loop (+ k 1) (compose-perm sigma current)))))

arguments.code:
;; σ is the identity permutation on {1,2,3,4}
;; Represent it as a function: every element maps to itself
(define (sigma x) x)

;; Compose permutations
(define (compose-perms f g)
  (lambda (x) (f (g x))))

;; Apply perm to a list to see its action
(define (apply-perm f lst)
  (map f lst))

(define domain '(1 2 3 4))

;; Check if a permutation is the identity
(define (identity? f)
  (equal? (apply-perm f domain) domain))

;; Compute order: smallest k >= 1 such that f^k = identity
(define (perm-order f)
  (let loop ((k 1) (fk f))
    (if (identity? fk)
        k
        (loop (+ k 1) (compose-perms f fk)))))

(display "sigma acts as: ")
(display (apply-perm sigma domain))
(newline)
(display "order of sigma: ")
(display (perm-order sigma))
(newline)

arguments.code:
;; Represent sigma = (2 3) as a vector: index i maps to sigma(i)
;; sigma swaps 2 and 3, fixes 1 and 4
;; Using 1-based: sigma(1)=1, sigma(2)=3, sigma(3)=2, sigma(4)=4
(define sigma '#(0 1 3 2 4))  ; index 0 unused

;; Compose two permutations (1-based, size 4)
(define (compose p q)
  (let ((result (make-vector 5 0)))
    (do ((i 1 (+ i 1)))
        ((> i 4) result)
      (vector-set! result i (vector-ref p (vector-ref q i))))))

;; Identity permutation
(define identity '#(0 1 2 3 4))

;; Check if two permutations are equal
(define (perm=? p q)
  (let loop ((i 1))
    (if (> i 4) #t
        (if (= (vector-ref p i) (vector-ref q i))
            (loop (+ i 1))
            #f))))

;; Compute order: find smallest k >= 1 such that sigma^k = identity
(define (order sigma)
  (let loop ((k 1) (current sigma))
    (if (perm=? current identity)
        k
        (loop (+ k 1) (compose sigma current)))))

(display "sigma = ") (display (vector->list sigma)) (newline)
(display "order of sigma = ") (display (order sigma)) (newline)

arguments.code:
;; Represent sigma as a vector (1-indexed via offset)
;; sigma = (1 3)(2 4): 1->3, 3->1, 2->4, 4->2
(define sigma '#(0 3 4 1 2))  ; index 0 unused

;; Apply permutation p to element x
(define (apply-perm p x) (vector-ref p x))

;; Compose two permutations on {1,2,3,4}
(define (compose-perm p q)
  (let ((r (make-vector 5 0)))
    (do ((i 1 (+ i 1)))
        ((> i 4) r)
      (vector-set! r i (apply-perm p (apply-perm q i))))))

;; Identity permutation
(define identity '#(0 1 2 3 4))

;; Check if permutation is identity
(define (is-identity? p)
  (and (= (vector-ref p 1) 1)
       (= (vector-ref p 2) 2)
       (= (vector-ref p 3) 3)
       (= (vector-ref p 4) 4)))

;; Compute the order of sigma
(let loop ((k 1) (current sigma))
  (if (is-identity? current)
      k
      (loop (+ k 1) (compose-perm sigma current))))

;; Verify: show sigma^1 and sigma^2
(define sigma2 (compose-perm sigma sigma))
(display "sigma^1: ") (display sigma) (newline)
(display "sigma^2: ") (display sigma2) (newline)
(display "sigma^2 is identity? ") (display (is-identity? sigma2)) (newline)

arguments.code:
;; Represent the permutation σ = (1 2 4) as a vector mapping.
;; σ sends: 1->2, 2->4, 4->1, and 3->3 (fixed).
;; We use 1-indexed lists.

(define (apply-perm perm x)
  ;; perm is a vector of length n+1 (1-indexed), perm[i] = σ(i)
  (vector-ref perm x))

(define (compose-perm perm1 perm2 n)
  ;; returns perm1 ∘ perm2 on {1..n}
  (let ((result (make-vector (+ n 1) 0)))
    (do ((i 1 (+ i 1)))
        ((> i n) result)
      (vector-set! result i (apply-perm perm1 (apply-perm perm2 i))))))

(define (identity-perm? perm n)
  (let loop ((i 1))
    (cond ((> i n) #t)
          ((not (= (vector-ref perm i) i)) #f)
          (else (loop (+ i 1))))))

;; σ = (1 2 4): 1->2, 2->4, 4->1, 3->3
(define sigma (vector 0 2 4 3 1))  ; index 0 unused

;; Compute powers of sigma until we hit identity
(let loop ((k 1) (current sigma))
  (display "sigma^") (display k) (display " = ")
  (display (cdr (vector->list current))) (newline)
  (if (identity-perm? current 4)
      (begin (display "Order = ") (display k) (newline))
      (loop (+ k 1) (compose-perm sigma current 4))))

- Result: 2 → 5

- α: 5 → 1

**Element 5:**

- α: 2 → 2 (not in α's cycle)
- Result: 5 → 2

α∘β: 1→4, 2→5, 3→3, 4→1, 5→2

In cycle notation:
- Start with 1: 1→4→1, giving (1 4)
- Start with 2: 2→5→2, giving (2 5)
- 3 is fixed

So α∘β = (1 4)(2 5)

## Step 3: Find the order

The composition (1 4)(2 5) is a product of two disjoint transpositions.

The order of a product of disjoint transpositions is lcm(2, 2) = **2**.

;; Represent permutations as vectors (1-indexed via offset)
;; α = (1 4 3 5) means 1->4, 4->3, 3->5, 5->1, 2->2
;; β = (2 3 4 5) means 2->3, 3->4, 4->5, 5->2, 1->1

;; Build permutation from cycle notation
(define (cycle->perm n cycle)
  ;; n = number of elements (1..n)
  ;; cycle = list of elements in the cycle
  (let ((perm (list->vector (iota (+ n 1))))) ; 0..n, use 1..n
    ;; set identity first
    (for-each (lambda (i) (vector-set! perm i i)) (iota n 1))
    ;; apply cycle: each element maps to the next, last maps to first
    (let loop ((c cycle))
      (if (null? (cdr c))
          (vector-set! perm (car c) (car cycle))
          (begin
            (vector-set! perm (car c) (cadr c))
            (loop (cdr c)))))
    perm))

;; Compose permutations: apply p2 first, then p1 (p1 ∘ p2)
(define (compose-perms n p1 p2)
  (let ((result (make-vector (+ n 1) 0)))
    (for-each (lambda (i)
                (vector-set! result i (vector-ref p1 (vector-ref p2 i))))
              (iota n 1))
    result))

;; Order of a permutation
(define (perm-order n p)
  (let ((identity (make-vector (+ n 1) 0)))
    (for-each (lambda (i) (vector-set! identity i i)) (iota n 1))
    (let loop ((power p) (k 1))
      (if (equal? (vector->list (map (lambda (i) (vector-ref power i)) (iota n 1)))
                  (map (lambda (i) (vector-ref identity i)) (iota n 1)))
          k
          (loop (compose-perms n p power) (+ k 1))))))

;; α = (1 4 3 5): 1->4, 4->3, 3->5, 5->1
(define alpha (cycle->perm 5 '(1 4 3 5)))
;; β = (2 3 4 5): 2->3, 3->4, 4->5, 5->2
(define beta (cycle->perm 5 '(2 3 4 5)))

(display "alpha: ") (display (map (lambda (i) (vector-ref alpha i)) '(1 2 3 4 5))) (newline)
(display "beta:  ") (display (map (lambda (i) (vector-ref beta i)) '(1 2 3 4 5))) (newline)

;; α∘β: apply β first, then α
(define alpha-beta (compose-perms 5 alpha beta))
(display "α∘β:   ") (display (map (lambda (i) (vector-ref alpha-beta i)) '(1 2 3 4 5))) (newline)

(display "Order of α∘β: ") (display (perm-order 5 alpha-beta)) (newline)

(import (srfi 1))

;; Build permutation from a single cycle
(define (cycle->perm n cycle)
  (let ((perm (make-vector (+ n 1) 0)))
    ;; identity first
    (let loop ((i 1))
      (when (<= i n)
        (vector-set! perm i i)
        (loop (+ i 1))))
    ;; apply cycle
    (let loop ((c cycle))
      (if (null? (cdr c))
          (vector-set! perm (car c) (car cycle))
          (begin
            (vector-set! perm (car c) (cadr c))
            (loop (cdr c)))))
    perm))

;; Compose: result(i) = p1(p2(i))
(define (compose-perms n p1 p2)
  (let ((result (make-vector (+ n 1) 0)))
    (let loop ((i 1))
      (when (<= i n)
        (vector-set! result i (vector-ref p1 (vector-ref p2 i)))
        (loop (+ i 1))))
    result))

;; Check if perm is identity
(define (perm-identity? n p)
  (let loop ((i 1))
    (if (> i n)
        #t
        (if (= (vector-ref p i) i)
            (loop (+ i 1))
            #f))))

;; Order of permutation
(define (perm-order n p)
  (let loop ((power p) (k 1))
    (if (perm-identity? n power)
        k
        (loop (compose-perms n p power) (+ k 1)))))

;; α = (1 4 3 5): 1->4, 4->3, 3->5, 5->1, 2->2
(define alpha (cycle->perm 5 '(1 4 3 5)))
;; β = (2 3 4 5): 2->3, 3->4, 4->5, 5->2, 1->1
(define beta (cycle->perm 5 '(2 3 4 5)))

(display "alpha: ") (display (map (lambda (i) (vector-ref alpha i)) '(1 2 3 4 5))) (newline)
(display "beta:  ") (display (map (lambda (i) (vector-ref beta i)) '(1 2 3 4 5))) (newline)

;; α∘β: apply β first, then α
(define alpha-beta (compose-perms 5 alpha beta))
(display "α∘β:   ") (display (map (lambda (i) (vector-ref alpha-beta i)) '(1 2 3 4 5))) (newline)

(display "Order of α∘β: ") (display (perm-order 5 alpha-beta)) (newline)

;; Let's verify by tracing α∘β step by step
;; β: 1->1, 2->3, 3->4, 4->5, 5->2
;; α: 1->4, 2->2, 3->5, 4->3, 5->1

;; α∘β(1) = α(β(1)) = α(1) = 4
;; α∘β(2) = α(β(2)) = α(3) = 5
;; α∘β(3) = α(β(3)) = α(4) = 3
;; α∘β(4) = α(β(4)) = α(5) = 1
;; α∘β(5) = α(β(5)) = α(2) = 2

;; So α∘β: 1->4, 2->5, 3->3, 4->1, 5->2
;; Written in cycle notation: (1 4)(2 5)(3) = (1 4)(2 5)
;; This is a product of two disjoint 2-cycles, so order = lcm(2,2) = 2

(display "Verification: α∘β maps:") (newline)
(for-each (lambda (i)
            (display i) (display " -> ")
            (display (vector-ref alpha-beta i)) (newline))
          '(1 2 3 4 5))
(display "Cycle structure: (1 4)(2 5) -- order = lcm(2,2) = 2") (newline)

;; Represent permutations as vectors (1-indexed, index 0 unused)
;; Identity permutation on S_5
(define (make-identity) (vector 0 1 2 3 4 5))

;; Apply permutation p to element x
(define (perm-apply p x) (vector-ref p x))

;; Compose: (compose p q) means apply q first, then p  => p∘q(x) = p(q(x))
(define (compose p q)
  (let ((result (make-vector 6 0)))
    (do ((i 1 (+ i 1)))
        ((> i 5) result)
      (vector-set! result i (perm-apply p (perm-apply q i))))))

;; Build a permutation from a list of cycles
(define (cycles->perm cycle-list)
  (let ((p (make-identity)))
    (for-each
      (lambda (cycle)
        (let ((len (length cycle)))
          (do ((i 0 (+ i 1)))
              ((= i len))
            (let ((from (list-ref cycle i))
                  (to   (list-ref cycle (modulo (+ i 1) len))))
              (vector-set! p from to)))))
      cycle-list)
    p))

;; α = (1 4 5): 1->4, 4->5, 5->1
(define alpha (cycles->perm '((1 4 5))))

;; β = (1 3)(2 4 5): 1->3, 3->1, 2->4, 4->5, 5->2
(define beta (cycles->perm '((1 3) (2 4 5))))

;; Compute α∘β (apply β first, then α)
(define alpha-beta (compose alpha beta))

;; Display the resulting permutation
(display "alpha: ") (display alpha) (newline)
(display "beta:  ") (display beta) (newline)
(display "alpha∘beta: ") (display alpha-beta) (newline)

;; Compute the order of a permutation: smallest k>0 such that p^k = identity
(define (perm-order p)
  (let loop ((current (compose p (make-identity))) (k 1))
    ;; check if current == identity
    (let ((is-id (let check ((i 1))
                   (if (> i 5) #t
                       (if (= (vector-ref current i) i)
                           (check (+ i 1))
                           #f)))))
      (if is-id
          k
          (loop (compose p current) (+ k 1))))))

;; Order of α∘β
(display "Order of alpha∘beta: ")
(display (perm-order alpha-beta))
(newline)

;; Let's also decompose into cycles to verify
(define (perm->cycles p)
  (let ((visited (make-vector 6 #f)))
    (vector-set! visited 0 #t)
    (let collect ((i 1) (cycles '()))
      (cond
        ((> i 5) (reverse cycles))
        ((vector-ref visited i) (collect (+ i 1) cycles))
        (else
          (let trace ((x i) (cycle '()))
            (if (vector-ref visited x)
                (if (> (length cycle) 1)
                    (collect (+ i 1) (cons (reverse cycle) cycles))
                    (collect (+ i 1) cycles))
                (begin
                  (vector-set! visited x #t)
                  (trace (vector-ref p x) (cons x cycle))))))))))

(display "Cycles of alpha∘beta: ")
(display (perm->cycles alpha-beta))
(newline)

;; Represent permutations as vectors (1-indexed via list, index 0 unused)
;; perm-from-cycles: takes a list of cycles, each cycle is a list of 1-based integers
;; Returns a vector of length 7 (indices 1-6 used)

(define (make-identity n)
  (let ((v (make-vector (+ n 1) 0)))
    (do ((i 1 (+ i 1)))
        ((> i n) v)
      (vector-set! v i i))))

(define (perm-from-cycles n cycles)
  (let ((v (make-identity n)))
    (for-each
      (lambda (cycle)
        (let ((len (length cycle)))
          (do ((k 0 (+ k 1)))
              ((= k len))
            (let ((from (list-ref cycle k))
                  (to   (list-ref cycle (modulo (+ k 1) len))))
              (vector-set! v from to)))))
      cycles)
    v))

;; Compose two permutations: (compose p q)(x) = p(q(x))  [apply q first, then p]
(define (perm-compose n p q)
  (let ((v (make-vector (+ n 1) 0)))
    (do ((i 1 (+ i 1)))
        ((> i n) v)
      (vector-set! v i (vector-ref p (vector-ref q i))))))

;; Inverse of a permutation
(define (perm-inverse n p)
  (let ((v (make-vector (+ n 1) 0)))
    (do ((i 1 (+ i 1)))
        ((> i n) v)
      (vector-set! v (vector-ref p i) i))))

;; Pretty-print one-line notation
(define (perm-oneline n p)
  (do ((i 1 (+ i 1)) (acc '() (cons (vector-ref p i) acc)))
      ((> i n) (reverse acc))))

;; --- Define α and β in S_6 ---
;; α = (1 2 5 3) as a 4-cycle
(define alpha (perm-from-cycles 6 '((1 2 5 3))))
;; β = (1 3 5 2 4 6) as a 6-cycle
(define beta  (perm-from-cycles 6 '((1 3 5 2 4 6))))

(display "alpha: ") (display (perm-oneline 6 alpha)) (newline)
(display "beta:  ") (display (perm-oneline 6 beta))  (newline)

;; α⁻¹ and β⁻¹
(define alpha-inv (perm-inverse 6 alpha))
(define beta-inv  (perm-inverse 6 beta))

(display "alpha-inv: ") (display (perm-oneline 6 alpha-inv)) (newline)
(display "beta-inv:  ") (display (perm-oneline 6 beta-inv))  (newline)

;; Commutator [α, β] = α ∘ β ∘ α⁻¹ ∘ β⁻¹
;; Apply right-to-left: β⁻¹ first, then α⁻¹, then β, then α
;; compose(p,q) = p after q, so we build left-to-right composition:
;; step1 = α⁻¹ ∘ β⁻¹  (apply β⁻¹ first)
;; step2 = β ∘ step1
;; step3 = α ∘ step2
(define step1 (perm-compose 6 alpha-inv beta-inv))
(define step2 (perm-compose 6 beta  step1))
(define commutator (perm-compose 6 alpha step2))

(display "commutator [α,β]: ") (display (perm-oneline 6 commutator)) (newline)

;; Verify by tracing each input element through β⁻¹ → α⁻¹ → β → α
(define (apply-perm p x) (vector-ref p x))

(for-each
  (lambda (i)
    (let* ((a (apply-perm beta-inv i))
           (b (apply-perm alpha-inv a))
           (c (apply-perm beta b))
           (d (apply-perm alpha c)))
      (display i) (display " -> β⁻¹: ") (display a)
      (display " -> α⁻¹: ") (display b)
      (display " -> β: ")   (display c)
      (display " -> α: ")   (display d) (newline)))
  '(1 2 3 4 5 6))

;; Represent permutations as vectors (1-indexed via offset)
;; perm-apply: applies permutation p to element x (1-based)
(define (perm-apply p x) (vector-ref p (- x 1)))

;; Compose permutations: (compose p q) means apply q first, then p
;; i.e. (p ∘ q)(x) = p(q(x))
(define (compose p q)
  (let ((n (vector-length p)))
    (let loop ((i 0) (result (make-vector n)))
      (if (= i n)
          result
          (begin
            (vector-set! result i
                         (perm-apply p (perm-apply q (+ i 1))))
            (loop (+ i 1) result))))))

;; Invert a permutation
(define (invert p)
  (let* ((n (vector-length p))
         (inv (make-vector n)))
    (do ((i 0 (+ i 1)))
        ((= i n) inv)
      (vector-set! inv (- (vector-ref p i) 1) (+ i 1)))))

;; Build a permutation from cycle notation (list of cycles)
;; Each cycle is a list of 1-based elements
(define (cycles->perm n cycles)
  ;; start with identity
  (let ((p (let loop ((i 1) (v (make-vector n)))
             (if (> i n) v
                 (begin (vector-set! v (- i 1) i) (loop (+ i 1) v))))))
    ;; apply each cycle
    (for-each
     (lambda (cyc)
       (let ((len (length cyc)))
         (do ((i 0 (+ i 1)))
             ((= i len))
           ;; cyc[i] -> cyc[(i+1) mod len]
           (let ((from (list-ref cyc i))
                 (to   (list-ref cyc (modulo (+ i 1) len))))
             (vector-set! p (- from 1) to)))))
     cycles)
    p))

;; α = (1 2)(3 6 5 4)
(define alpha (cycles->perm 6 '((1 2) (3 6 5 4))))
;; β = (1 4 6 3 5)  — 2 is a fixed point
(define beta  (cycles->perm 6 '((1 4 6 3 5))))

;; Inverses
(define alpha-inv (invert alpha))
(define beta-inv  (invert beta))

;; Commutator [α,β] = α ∘ β ∘ α⁻¹ ∘ β⁻¹
;; Applying right-to-left: β⁻¹ first, then α⁻¹, then β, then α
;; compose is "left ∘ right", so:
(define commutator
  (compose alpha
           (compose beta
                    (compose alpha-inv beta-inv))))

;; Display each permutation for verification
(display "alpha:     ") (display (vector->list alpha))     (newline)
(display "beta:      ") (display (vector->list beta))      (newline)
(display "alpha-inv: ") (display (vector->list alpha-inv)) (newline)
(display "beta-inv:  ") (display (vector->list beta-inv))  (newline)
(display "commutator [a,b] = a b a^-1 b^-1: ")
(display (vector->list commutator)) (newline)

;; Verify each permutation against the cycle descriptions manually
;; α = (1 2)(3 6 5 4): 1->2, 2->1, 3->6, 6->5, 5->4, 4->3
;; Expected one-line: (2 1 6 3 4 5) ✓

;; β = (1 4 6 3 5): 1->4, 4->6, 6->3, 3->5, 5->1, 2 fixed
;; Expected one-line: (4 2 5 6 1 3) ✓

;; Step-by-step commutator trace on each element
(for-each
 (lambda (x)
   (let* ((step1 (perm-apply beta-inv x))
          (step2 (perm-apply alpha-inv step1))
          (step3 (perm-apply beta step2))
          (step4 (perm-apply alpha step3)))
     (display x) (display " -> β⁻¹: ") (display step1)
     (display " -> α⁻¹: ") (display step2)
     (display " -> β: ")   (display step3)
     (display " -> α: ")   (display step4)
     (newline)))
 '(1 2 3 4 5 6))

;; Build permutation from cycle notation
(define (cycles->perm cycles n)
  ;; Start with identity
  (let ((p (list->vector (cons 0 (iota n 1)))))  ; p[i] = i for i=1..n
    (for-each
      (lambda (cyc)

          (for-each

            (iota len))))

;; Compose permutations: (compose p q)(x) = p(q(x))  -- apply q first, then p
(define (compose p q)
  (let* ((n (- (vector-length p) 1))
         (r (make-vector (+ n 1) 0)))
    (do ((i 1 (+ i 1)))
        ((> i n) r)
      (vector-set! r i (vector-ref p (vector-ref q i))))))

  (let* ((n (- (vector-length p) 1))
         (r (make-vector (+ n 1) 0)))
    (do ((i 1 (+ i 1)))
        ((> i n) r)
      (vector-set! r (vector-ref p i) i))))

  (let ((n (- (vector-length p) 1)))

;; Use srfi-1 or build our own iota
(define (my-iota n . args)
  (let ((start (if (null? args) 0 (car args))))
    (let loop ((i 0) (acc '()))
      (if (= i n)
          (reverse acc)
          (loop (+ i 1) (cons (+ start i) acc))))))

;; Build permutation from cycle notation
(define (cycles->perm cycles n)
  (let ((p (list->vector (cons 0 (my-iota n 1)))))
    (for-each
      (lambda (cyc)
        (let ((len (length cyc)))
          (for-each
            (lambda (k)
              (let ((from (list-ref cyc k))
                    (to   (list-ref cyc (modulo (+ k 1) len))))
                (vector-set! p from to)))
            (my-iota len))))
      cycles)
    p))

(define (compose p q)
  (let* ((n (- (vector-length p) 1))
         (r (make-vector (+ n 1) 0)))
    (do ((i 1 (+ i 1)))
        ((> i n) r)
      (vector-set! r i (vector-ref p (vector-ref q i))))))

    (do ((i 1 (+ i 1)))

      (vector-set! r (vector-ref p i) i))))
